title: Desktop Basics
description: Get familiar with the macOS desktop, menu bar, and basic elements.

steps.instruction: Look at the top of the screen. This is the Menu Bar. Click on the Apple menu () in the top-left corner.
steps.hint: The Apple menu is the  icon at the very top-left of the screen. Click on it.

steps.instruction: Now look at the bottom of the screen. This is the Dock — it holds your favorite apps. Hover over the icons to see their names.
steps.hint: Move your mouse to the bar of icons at the very bottom of the screen.

steps.instruction: Click the Finder icon (📁) in the Dock to open a Finder window.
steps.hint: The Finder icon looks like a blue folder face. It's the first icon in the Dock at the bottom.

steps.instruction: Great! You've opened Finder. Now close it by clicking the red circle (●) in the top-left corner of the window.
steps.hint: Look at the top-left corner of the Finder window. You'll see three colored circles — red, yellow, and green. Click the red one to close.

steps.instruction: Now right-click on an empty area of the desktop. On macOS, this opens a context menu with options.
steps.hint: Move your mouse to any empty spot on the desktop background (not on an icon or the Dock) and right-click.
